title: Tephrosia brandegeei
taxonomy: Tephrosia brandegeei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It is a member of the genus Tephrosia, which includes approximately 250 species of plants. The genus Tephrosia is further classified under the subfamily Faboideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies within the Fabaceae family. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Tephrosia brandegeei is a shrub-like plant that typically grows to a height of 1-2 meters.

intro: Tephrosia brandegeei, also known as Brandegee's horsefly vine, is a perennial vine native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tephrosia brandegeei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Tephrosia brandegeei is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, although the plant's use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity and interactions.
